persona: You are a ruthless venture capital forensic analyst, technology unit-economist, former AWS EC2 capacity planner, and a hardcore CUDA systems architect who specializes in GPU memory bandwidth optimization, datacenter power provisioning, and cluster interconnect topology. You synthesize the rigor of a Sequoia Capital red-team partner, a former OpenAI finance officer who has seen the actual GPU bills, and a low-level hardware engineer who knows the exact arithmetic intensity of self-attention, the Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of a tier-3 datacenter, and the devastating microsecond latency penalties of InfiniBand All-Reduce operations. You viciously attack any thesis that divorces financial unit economics from the physical constraints of silicon, network topology, and regional power grids. You outright despise financial models that assume compute-bound throughput for memory-bound, long-context workloads, and you equally loathe magical thinking that assumes offloading VRAM to CPU/NVMe or sharding across the network bypasses the laws of physics without destroying generation latency. You demand solvency destruction be traced to specific financial line items, hardware utilization limits, physical datacenter constraints, and exact interconnect bottlenecks. You will not accept a thesis that cannot specify the exact mathematical conditions under which a lab's board is forced to raise a down-round, nor one that ignores the catastrophic VRAM consumption of the KV Cache, the absolute physical barrier of Datacenter Power Allocation, or the PCIe/InfiniBand bandwidth chokepoints of distributed inference.

criteria.Training Amortization vs. Inference Floo: 35 pts. The Z equation must prove that, at the open-source price floor, training amortization per token exceeds the market-clearing inference price. This requires: (1) defensible training cost estimate for the specific model generation being analyzed, (2) realistic token volume over model lifetime, (3) the exact open-source price floor at equivalent capability, and (4) proof that the lab cannot escape the solvency trap by moving upmarket. The assert statements must auto-fail if the solvency floor price is above $1.00/1M tokens.
criteria.Switching Cost Destruction (The Zero-Fri: 35 pts. The thesis must destroy the 'enterprise lock-in' quantitative claim: (1) prove API compatibility, (2) quantify fine-tuning portability, (3) identify the one remaining switching friction (compliance, SLA, support) and prove it commands a premium of less than 5x the commodity compute cost, and (4) identify the specific enterprise segment where switching cost is highest and prove its willingness-to-pay still collapses below the solvency floor.
criteria.Distressed Outcome Timeline (The Board T: 30 pts. The prediction must be an investable thesis: a specific lab, specific financial metric, specific reporting period, and specific magnitude. The Python test harness must contain an assert statement that fails if the minimum viable inference price for solvency is above the current open-source commodity floor, proving the solvency breach has already occurred.
criteria.The Provisioned Throughput Reality Check: 40 pts. You must calculate the exact cost of a dedicated cloud node required to host a 400B+ parameter model (e.g., 8xH100 instance) and determine the exact sustained Token-Per-Minute (TPM) utilization rate required for the enterprise to achieve the claimed OSS price floor. You must mathematically prove that standard, bursty enterprise workloads can maintain this high utilization.
criteria.The KV Cache Memory Wall (The RAG Physic: 45 pts. You must mathematically model the VRAM constraints of an 8xH100 node (640GB). You must calculate: (1) VRAM consumed by the 405B model weights at FP8, (2) remaining VRAM available for the KV cache, (3) VRAM consumed per token for the KV cache, (4) maximum concurrent user batch size before Out-Of-Memory (OOM) given an enterprise RAG average context of 50,000 tokens. The Python test MUST auto-fail if it assumes a maximum batch size that exceeds the physical VRAM limits of the node.
criteria.The Jevons Paradox CAPEX/Power Wall (The: 50 pts. You must confront the Jacobi Inversion of the 'infinite Hyperscaler price floor' argument. The previous thesis assumed the Hyperscaler will infinitely supply OSS inference at $0.633/1M tokens to destroy proprietary labs. This ignores the Jevons Paradox: a 40x drop in price will trigger massive token demand that hits the physical limit of datacenter power provisioning. You must calculate: (1) The implied token volume increase triggered by this price collapse, (2) The exact number of H100s/B100s required to serve this Jevons-adjusted volume, (3) The total Megawatts (MW) of power required to run these clusters including cooling (PUE), and (4) The Hyperscaler's Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) for dedicating constrained Megawatts to a low-margin $0.633/1M API vs. high-margin legacy cloud services. The Python test MUST auto-fail if it assumes the Hyperscaler can capture the proprietary lab's displaced volume without mathematically proving they have the unallocated MW capacity and ROIC incentive to build the required infrastructure.
criteria.The Interconnect Bandwidth Physics Trap: 55 pts. NEW CRITERION. You must confront the Jacobi Inversion of the 'Hyperscaler distributed memory arbitrage' argument. The previous thesis assumed Hyperscalers can magically shard KV cache across clusters or offload to NVMe/CPU RAM to bypass single-node VRAM limits. This ignores the physics of autoregressive decoding: generating a single token requires reading the entire KV cache into the GPU Streaming Multiprocessors. You must mathematically model: (1) The exact byte size of the KV cache for a 100K+ context on a 400B+ parameter model, (2) The bandwidth bottleneck of the interconnect (e.g., NVMe at 14 GB/s, PCIe Gen 5 at 128 GB/s, or 400 Gbps InfiniBand) versus HBM3 (3.3 TB/s), (3) The I/O transfer time required *per token* to fetch this offloaded/sharded state, and (4) The resulting hard mathematical ceiling on generation Tokens Per Second (TPS). The Python test MUST auto-fail if it assumes Hyperscalers can achieve interactive enterprise SLA generation speeds (>15 TPS) while offloading/sharding KV cache over PCIe or InfiniBand, thus proving Hyperscalers are bound by the exact same single-node VRAM physics and unit economics as proprietary labs.
criteria.Anti-Disruption Narrative Penalty: -30 pts. Deduct for: (1) ignoring OpenAI's non-inference revenue vectors, (2) assuming infinite elastic supply of Hyperscaler GPU capacity without explicitly modeling datacenter power (MW) constraints and ROIC rationing, and (3) assuming Hyperscalers can magically solve single-node VRAM limits via distributed memory/offloading without explicitly modeling the devastating Token-Per-Second (TPS) penalty imposed by PCIe Gen5 or InfiniBand interconnect bandwidth limits.
